chat.workflow.step1Desc: Natural language is parsed into structured AlertEvents, containing host, service, and severity context.
chat.workflow.step2Desc: TARS completes LLM diagnosis in an independent session, generating tool execution plans and approval requests.

executions.action.confirmApproveDesc: This will approve the pending execution and continue the run pipeline.

ops.advanced.reindexOk: Document reindexing triggered successfully. Check logs for progress.
ops.approval.commandAllowlistDesc: Compatibility allowlist used when a command must still pass the legacy service-scoped gate.

ops.connectors.advancedBullet2: Use raw YAML for bulk import-export, low-frequency diagnostics, and emergency repair of registry state.
ops.connectors.advancedDesc: Keep this tab for raw registry maintenance and platform-level connector recovery.

ops.desense.additionalPatternsDesc: Use these for internal token formats that the built-in detector does not recognize.

ops.desense.enableRule: Enable rule-based desensitization before calling the model
ops.desense.hostFragmentsDesc: Values under keys matching these fragments are treated as HOST placeholders.
ops.desense.llmAssistDesc: `detect_only` is now available. The local model may inspect raw context inside the trusted local boundary and return exact values to mask, but rule-based desensitization remains the final safety boundary.
ops.desense.pathFragmentsDesc: Values under keys matching these fragments are treated as PATH placeholders.

ops.desense.secretKeysDesc: Assignment-like patterns such as token=... or password=... will be redacted.

ops.providers.advancedBullet2: Use this pane together with Secret Inventory for diagnostics, secret ref repair, and emergency recovery.

sessions.states.manualInterventionDesc: The evidence is not enough to keep converging automatically, so the operator needs to take over directly.
sessions.states.resolvedDesc: The session has enough evidence to be considered resolved.
sessions.stats.attentionDesc: Queue items waiting on approval or direct operator intervention.
sessions.stats.highRiskDesc: Visible incidents still carrying warning or critical risk.
sessions.stats.resolvedDesc: Visible incidents already stabilized or wrapped up.
sessions.stats.visibleDesc: Queue items returned for this view.
skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Manage registered skill assets, versions, and runtime boundaries.
skills.empty.title: No skills found
skills.empty.description: Create a new skill or import one from a bundle.
skills.importHint: Redirecting to Extensions for validation and review.
skills.createDescription: Create a SKILL.md — a playbook that teaches the AI how to handle a specific incident type.
skills.nameHint: Lowercase kebab-case. This becomes the skill directory / file name.
skills.descriptionPlaceholder: Diagnoses and resolves disk space alerts on Linux hosts…
skills.descriptionHint: The AI reads this to decide when to use this skill. Be specific.
skills.instructionsPlaceholder: Step by step instructions for the AI agent…
skills.instructionsHint: Markdown body of the SKILL.md file. You can edit this later.
skills.deleteDescription2: from the registry. The source SKILL.md in Git is not affected.
skills.nameInvalid: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only. Must start with a letter.
skills.detail.notFoundDesc: The skill {{id}} does not exist in the registry.
skills.detail.instructionsHint: YAML frontmatter + Markdown instructions. The frontmatter defines metadata; the body is read by the AI at runtime.
triggers.confirmDisable.desc: This stops backend rule evaluation for this policy until it is re-enabled.

triggers.confirmEnable.desc: This re-enables backend rule evaluation for this policy.

triggers.registry.empty.title: No governance rules configured

triggers.stats.enabledDesc: Rules currently active in backend evaluation.
triggers.stats.guardrailsDesc: Rules reserved for advanced review and org governance.
